name: background-job-template
description: Pattern for fire-and-forget background jobs in a single-instance Next.js app: in-memory job store, GET/POST API route, and a polling button component that renders four states (idle/working/done/error). Replace placeholders to fit your concrete job kind.

placeholders.ENTITY: PascalCase singular for the job kind (e.g. Download, ImportJob, Transcode)
placeholders.entities: plural lowercase / kebab-case (e.g. downloads, import-jobs, transcodes)

endpoints: GET  /api/{{entities}}/<id> — current job state, or { status: 'idle' }
POST /api/{{entities}}/<id> — start a new job, returns { status: 'started' }

postInstall: 1. Pick a concrete job kind (Download / ImportJob / Transcode / ...)
3. Replace the body of the run<Entity>(id, req) function in the API route with your actual long-running work — call set<Entity>Job(id, { status: 'running', progress: ... }) with updates
4. Use the button: <DownloadButton jobId={mediaId} /> — it polls /api/downloads/<id> every 2s until done or error
5. The store is in-memory; if you scale beyond one Node.js process, swap lib/<entity>-store.ts for Redis / DB persistence
6. Optionally call gc<Entity>Jobs() periodically to drop terminal jobs older than 1 hour
